title: symplocos pulchra subsp. villosa
intro: Symplocos pulchra subsp. villosa, also known as the Philippine syzygium or hairy syzygium, is a plant species that belongs to the family Symplocaceae and is native to the Philippines. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Symplocos pulchra subsp. villosa can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or extract, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
